hook: You've rephrased the same prompt 6 times and it still sounds nothing like you.
caption:
You've rephrased the same prompt 6 times and it still sounds nothing like you.

Here's what's actually happening:

AI doesn't know if you're the person who needs three bullet points and a TL;DR — or the person who will literally never read a bullet point and needs it written like a conversation.

It doesn't know you hate the word 'leverage.' That you always want the counterargument included. That 'comprehensive' outputs stress you out and you'd rather have one sharp idea than seven okay ones.

So it guesses. And it guesses... average.

The fix isn't a better prompt. It's a better starting point.

When AI knows you default to systems thinking, hate hedging language, and process information better through analogies than abstractions — the first response lands. Not the seventh.

That's what a BootFile does. It's not a prompt template. It's a profile of how your brain actually works, so AI stops writing for everyone and starts writing for you.

Stop rewording. Start calibrating.
